title: Psychotria suarezensis
intro: Psychotria suarezensis, also known as Madagascar tea tree, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to the island of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, diarrhea, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria suarezensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to promote digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system.
taxonomy: Psychotria suarezensis is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Within this family, Psychotria suarezensis is part of the genus Psychotria, which contains approximately 1,800 species of plants. The genus Psychotria is further divided into several subgenera and sections. The classification of Psychotria suarezensis is still a subject of ongoing research and may be revised as new information becomes available.
